data.narrative:
LeydenJar Technologies is a Series B-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector, having raised €23M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 6 same-sector, same-stage companies, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), supplemented by one public comparable (ticker LNZA).

The peer-median method, the primary triangulation approach applicable here, yields a median valuation of $355M across the six-company set. The peer universe spans a wide range from $120M to $5.0B, reflecting the heterogeneity of venture-stage climate technology valuations. This range suggests material variation in investor expectations or company-specific risk profiles within the sector. The median figure of $355M anchors the central estimate.

Based on this single-method analysis, the triangulated valuation range is $302M (low), $355M (median), and $408M (high). The primary uncertainty stems from the limited methodological diversity—only peer-median comparison was applicable with the available data—and the substantial valuation dispersion within the comparable set, particularly the presence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B. Validation of this range would benefit from additional methods (discounted cash flow, revenue multiples) and further specification of LeydenJar's relative technology maturity and capital intensity versus peers.
